cot_prefix: Okay, so I have this problem here about an 8×8 chessboard with two diagonally opposite corners removed. The question is whether the remaining 62 squares can be perfectly tiled with 31 dominoes, each covering two adjacent squares. Hmm, interesting. Let me try to figure this out step by step. First, I know that a standard domino covers exactly two squares. Since the chessboard originally has 64 squares, removing two leaves 62 squares, which would require 31 dominoes to cover if it's possible. So the first thing that comes to mind is the classic problem where you have a chessboard with two squares removed and trying to tile it with dominoes. I remember there are some parity or coloring arguments involved here. Let me recall: a standard chessboard has alternating black and white squares, right? Each domino, when placed on the board, will always cover one black and one white square, no matter how it's placed—horizontally or vertically.
